question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper @Mapping(source = 'customFee', target = 'customFees') singular-to-plural mapping to trigger a scenario where a topic with multiple custom fees only has one returned (due to the single CustomFee object mapping), causing clients to underpay by missing additional required fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of output encoding validation in the Topic REST model returned by topicMapper.map() to inject malformed fee amounts (e.g., negative values, NaN, Infinity) that cause client-side fee calculation errors leading to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter.valueOf() static factory method's lack of upper-bound validation on the num field to supply num=0, causing a lookup for entity 0.0.0 which may be a system entity with special fee properties, and use that data to mislead clients about fee requirements?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getTopic() method does not validate that all three service responses (topic, entity, customFee) refer to the same logical entity before passing them to topicMapper.map(), allowing a data inconsistency to produce a misleading fee response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @RequiredArgsConstructor injection of customFeeService, entityService, topicMapper, and topicService to perform a dependency confusion attack if any of these beans can be overridden via classpath manipulation, causing a malicious service implementation to return crafted fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of pagination or field filtering on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to receive the full custom fee structure including all fee collector accounts, enabling targeted social engineering attacks against fee collectors to redirect fee payments?
